channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Parliament Energy Committee Launches Inquiry into Fuel Quality and Import Irregularities in Kenya
item.description: The National Assembly Committee on Energy in Kenya has initiated a detailed fact-finding mission aimed at addressing growing concerns over fuel shortages and the quality of imported petroleum products...

item.title: China Criticises US Blockade of Iranian Ports Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: China has sharply criticised the United States for its recent blockade of Iranian ports, describing the move as 'dangerous and irresponsible.' The blockade, implemented despite a fragile two-week ceas...

item.title: Tankers Navigate Strait of Hormuz Despite US Blockade on Iranian Ports
item.description: Despite a newly imposed US blockade on ports in Iran, several vessels, including those sanctioned by the US, successfully passed through the crucial Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, April 14th, 2026. This...

item.title: Simone Giertz Designs a Chair Specifically for Temporary Clothing Storage
item.description: Simone Giertz has taken a common household phenomenon—the 'clothes chair'—and turned it into a purposeful piece of furniture. Many homes have a chair that collects clothes worn once: too clean for the...

item.title: Nyong'o urges swift action to restore security in Kisumu after attacks on public figures and businesses
item.description: Kisumu Governor Anyang' Nyong'o has strongly condemned recent violent incidents targeting private property and public safety in Kisumu. The incidents, which involved attacks on Fairways Hotel and Java...

item.title: Column 8 letters: Music, lunar missions, and kookaburras
item.description: The latest Column 8 edition of The Sydney Morning Herald features a diverse collection of letters from readers across Australia. Topics range from playful commentary on political figures, music, and l...

item.title: Wall Street Banks Benefit From Increased Market Volatility
item.description: Recent market turbulence has complicated life for many investors, but big Wall Street banks are finding unexpected advantages amid the chaos. Trading volumes have surged significantly as investors rep...

item.title: Mwanza City to Develop Modern Park, Celebrates New Mkuyuni Bridge
item.description: Mwanza city is set to get a brand new, modern park near the Mkuyuni Bridge, according to government plans. The project, spearheaded by TANROADS (Tanzania National Roads Agency), aims to transform the ...

item.title: Lagos State Fully Automates Housing Planning Permits
item.description: Lagos State don finally move all housing planning permit processes to digital mode. According to Dr. Olajide Abiodun, Special Adviser to the Governor on E-GIS and Urban Development, the state don comp...

item.title: Tanzania Turns to Public-Private Partnerships to Drive National Development
item.description: Tanzania is making a strategic move to accelerate its national development through Public-Private Partnerships (PPPs), a key element of the Fourth Five-Year Development Plan (FYDP IV). The plan requir...

item.title: House Committee Subpoenas Sonny Trillanes and AMLC for Duterte Impeachment Hearing
item.description: The House Committee on Justice has issued a subpoena requiring former Senator Antonio Trillanes IV to appear at the upcoming impeachment hearing of Vice President Sara Duterte on April 22, 2026. The s...

item.title: 87-year-old bushwalker survives cold night in WA using only raisins
item.description: An 87-year-old bushwalker in Western Australia's south-west survived a night in near-freezing conditions by carefully rationing a small packet of raisins. The incident highlights the risks that even e...

item.title: Karen Kennedy completes historic quadruple Robben Island crossing in cold Atlantic waters
item.description: Karen Kennedy, a 59-year-old former provincial swimmer, has made history by completing a gruelling quadruple Robben Island crossing without wearing a wetsuit. The challenging 30km swim took place in t...

item.title: U.S. Stocks Steady as Investors Digest Inflation Data and Earnings Reports
item.description: On April 13, 2026, the Wall Street Journal’s live coverage detailed the movement of U.S. stock indexes throughout the day. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite were relative...

item.title: Concerns raised over perceived politicization of Canada’s judicial appointment process
item.description: In this opinion piece, lawyer Collin May argues that Canada’s judicial appointment system has become increasingly politicized and lacks transparency, particularly through the role of judicial advisory...

item.title: DCI Reveals How RIAT Student Rose Benter Apondi Was Brutally Killed by Ex-Boyfriend in Kisumu
item.description: Detectives from the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) in Kisumu have released shocking details surrounding the murder of 21-year-old Rose Benter Apondi, a student at Ramogi Institute of Adv...

item.title: Several iconic British musicians join the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame in a landmark year
item.description: This year's Rock & Roll Hall of Fame ceremony is set to make history with an unprecedented number of British artists among the new inductees. The official announcement highlights the enduring global i...

item.title: Tanzania prepares for Slovak Foreign Minister visit to boost diplomatic and economic cooperation
item.description: Today, April 14, 2026, in Dar es Salaam, the Slovak Ambassador to Tanzania, Ivan Lančarič, had a meeting with Ellen Maduhu, Acting Director of the Europe and Americas Department in the Ministry of For...

item.title: Amherst, N.S. man fatally shot in local incident
item.description: Authorities in Amherst, Nova Scotia, are investigating after a man was found dead following a shooting incident. The police have confirmed that the circumstances surrounding the death are suspicious, ...

item.title: French Government Considers Blocking Kanye West Concert in Marseille Over Antisemitic Remarks
item.description: The French government dey look to stop US rapper Kanye West from performing for Marseille because of him antisemitic comments. Interior Minister Laurent Nunez dey determined to block the June 11 conce...
